articles.title: Asia Stocks to Gain, Oil Rises on US-Iran Deadlock: Markets Wrap - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: A retreat in high-flying technology stocks is dragging equities lower as traders wait for US inflation data. Brent rose for a third straight day, with the ceasefire in the Middle East appearing increasingly fragile.

articles.title: Clarity Act, in the flesh, unveiled by U.S. Senate Banking Committee before hearing - CoinDesk
articles.description: The crypto market structure bill had already been making the industry rounds behind closed doors, but the lawmakers have released the text before their vote.

articles.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Closes at Record High as AI Trade Accelerates - TipRanks
articles.description: The chipmaker reports earnings on May 20.

articles.title: Fake Trump and Oprah ads fuel a wave of Medicare scams on Facebook, report says - NBC News
articles.description: Meta, the parent of Facebook and Instagram, is failing to stop scammers who repeatedly buy ads on the platforms to target seniors with scams, a new report says.

articles.title: Byron Allen strikes deal to buy controlling stake in BuzzFeed and become CEO - Axios
articles.description: The deal gives the 20-year-old digital publisher a much-needed lifeline. Without a buyer, the company likely would've been forced to file for bankruptcy.

articles.title: Cell phone data shows Trump's trade policies are hurting more than just Canadian tourism - Business Insider
articles.description: Researchers at the University of Toronto found Canadian visits to US cities fell by nearly half in the year following the start of Trump's trade war.

articles.title: Andrew Morse to step down, Paul Curran named AJC president and publisher - AJC.com
articles.description: Cox Enterprises names Paul Curran president and publisher of The Atlanta Journal-Constitution to sustain its journalism and continue digital transformation.

articles.title: Stock futures are little changed as traders await inflation reading, monitor Iran war developments: Live updates - CNBC
articles.description: Economists polled by Dow Jones are calling for headline CPI to have gained 3.7% from a year earlier.

articles.title: Netflix sued by Texas AG for alleged surveillance, addictive features - Politico
articles.description: The complaint follows a product liability playbook that has been successful against other big tech platforms.

articles.title: Here’s how to get the U.S. Mint’s $1 coin honoring Steve Jobs - 9to5Mac
articles.description: Tomorrow, May 12, marks the public sale of the U.S. Mint’s latest American Innovation $1 coin, which honors Steve Jobs as California’s representative in the program.

articles.title: Nvidia’s CEO to Miss China Trip After Year of Travels With Trump - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: The roster of business leaders invited to join President Donald Trump on his trip to China this week left off one notable, and surprising, name: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang.

articles.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills - TechCrunch
articles.description: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

articles.title: 4 ways Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella threw cold water on Elon Musk's case - Business Insider
articles.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella was called to the stand Monday at the ongoing Musk-v-Altman trial. But Nadella may have hurt Musk more than helped him.

articles.title: After taking $100 deposits, Trump Mobile changes its terms to say the Trump phone may never be made - Fortune
articles.description: The terms and conditions said the preorder deposit gives people "a conditional opportunity" to buy the phone if the company makes it.

articles.title: I Sat In The New Audi Q9—It Raises The Bar For Audi Interiors - Motor1.com
articles.description: The new 2027 Audi Q9 has a lavish interior with three rows worth of seating. It raises the bar on the brand's cabins.

articles.title: Stocks are hitting record highs even as Iran war drags on. How long can it last? - MarketWatch
articles.description: The S&P 500 finished above 7,400 for the first time ever but Wall Street’s ‘fear gauge’ also spiked higher

articles.title: Micron and SK Hynix Stocks Surge. Thank Trouble at Samsung. - Barron's
articles.description: Micron and SK Hynix stocks rose Monday. Memory-chip rival Samsung could be about to take a hit to production.

articles.title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water and nobody noticed for months - Ars Technica
articles.description: Can AI save us from the AI industry’s endless thirst for water? Outlook not so good.
